examples.title: Breadth-First Search (BFS)
examples.description: Implementation of BFS for finding shortest path in unweighted graph
examples.explanation: BFS uses a FIFO queue to explore all nodes at depth d before depth d+1, guaranteeing shortest path in unweighted graphs.

examples.title: A* Search Algorithm
examples.explanation: A* combines actual path cost g(n) with heuristic estimate h(n) to find optimal solution. Optimal if h is admissible.

examples.title: Minimax Algorithm
examples.description: Game tree search for two-player zero-sum games
examples.explanation: Minimax recursively evaluates game trees assuming optimal play. MAX tries to maximize score, MIN tries to minimize.

examples.title: Alpha-Beta Pruning
examples.explanation: Alpha-Beta pruning eliminates branches that cannot influence the final decision, reducing search time from O(b^d) to O(b^(d/2)) with perfect ordering.

examples.title: Hill Climbing Search
examples.explanation: Hill Climbing is a local search algorithm that continuously moves to the neighbor with highest value. Simple but can get stuck in local maxima.
examples.timeComplexity: O(d) where d is distance to local maximum
